checks.a_analysis: I have carefully compared the text content of the JSON output against the source PDF. The JSON correctly identifies headings and table data from the pages it processed. There are no instances of fabricated paragraphs or sentences that do not exist in the source material.

I verified the URLs and email addresses present in the JSON against the source PDF. The URLs for FCC guides on pages 3 and 4 are correct. The email address 'firsttech@nc.gov' on page 26 is correct. No fabricated contact information was found.

The JSON output contains a explicit omission placeholder indicating that 20 sections (pages) were omitted for token budget reasons. Specifically, pages 5 through 23, which contain maps, graphics, and text about the Homework Gap and Broadband Service Inventory, are entirely missing. Page 25, regarding the First Responder program, is also missing. This constitutes a significant loss of document integrity.
checks.b_evidence_source: Pages 5-23 containing maps (Broadband Service Inventory, Adoption Rates) and slides regarding barriers, the Homework Gap study, and the GREAT Grant program details. Page 25 containing details on the First Responder Emerging Technologies Program.

checks.a_analysis: The tables on pages 3 (Broadband Speeds), 4 (Typical Broadband Usage), and 24 (GREAT Grant Program) were checked. The row counts in the JSON match the data rows in the source PDF. The table on page 24 correctly lists all 19 awardees.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There are major text omissions within the sections that were included. On page 2 (Broadband Technology Types), every technology listed (DSL, Cable Modem, Fiber, Wireless, Satellite) is missing its descriptive sub-bullets containing speed ranges and technical specifications. Additionally, on page 27 (Let's Connect!), the social media handles (e.g., @NCDIT, @BroadbandIO) and the department website (it.nc.gov) are present as text in the PDF but completely missing from the JSON output.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2 text under bullets: 'Runs over traditional copper phone lines...', 'Speeds range from 1.5 Mbps to 80 Mbps', etc. Page 27 text: '@NCDIT', '@BroadbandIO', '@ncicenter', 'it.nc.gov'.
checks.b_evidence_output: List items on page 2 only contain the headers (e.g., 'Digital Subscriber Line (DSL)') without the accompanying technical text. Page 27 JSON contains only images/headings, missing handles and URLs.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: In the sections processed, the wording and numerical values (speeds, Mbps ranges) accurately reflect the source PDF. The only issues are omissions (C5) rather than incorrect transcriptions.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output and compared it to the source PDF. The text content and images appear in the output only as many times as they appear in the source document. No unintended duplications were found.

The heading hierarchy is correctly preserved. Major page titles are assigned H1 (e.g., 'What is Broadband?', 'Broadband Technology Types'), and subsections like 'GREAT Awards – May 2019' are correctly nested as H2. No heading levels are skipped.

The tables on Page 3 ('Broadband Speeds'), Page 4 ('Typical Broadband Usage'), and Page 24 (GREAT Awards) are structurally sound. Columns and rows correctly correspond to the source material. Section headers within tables (like 'General Usage' on Page 3) are represented as single-cell rows, which is an acceptable structural interpretation.
The output follows the logical flow of the source PDF, processing pages and elements in sequential order from top to bottom.

The bulleted list on Page 2 ('Broadband Technology Types') has been incorrectly converted. While the top-level items are identified, all descriptive sub-bullets (the lines starting with hyphens in the source, such as '- Runs over traditional copper...') have been omitted from the list items. This results in a loss of the list's meaning and internal structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2 bullet points under 'Digital Subscriber Line (DSL)', 'Cable Modem', etc., containing descriptive details starting with hyphens (e.g., '- Runs over traditional copper phone lines...').
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 2, list item index 8 contains only the top-level technology names (e.g., 'Digital Subscriber Line (DSL)') without the associated descriptions.

checks.a_analysis: There are several missing links/URLs in the output. The source URL at the bottom of Page 4 is truncated, losing the clickable link text. Additionally, all contact handles (e.g., @NCDIT) and the website URL (it.nc.gov) on Page 27 are missing from the text elements, though they are clearly visible in the source PDF.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 4, reading_order 19 text: 'Source: '. Section 27 elements missing handles and URL.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output follows the linear sequence of the original presentation slides. Maps, tables, and lists are presented in the same relative order as the source PDF. While decorative graphics are simplified, the visual organization of information is preserved.

The tables on pages 3 (Broadband Speeds), 4 (Typical Broadband Usage), and 24 (GREAT Grant Program) are rendered correctly as structured HTML tables with clear row and column alignment, making them readable and understandable.

Several meaningful infographic elements from the source PDF are missing or broken in the HTML conversion. On page 10, the hexagon graphics and the text 'Capital Expenditures (CAPX)' are missing, replaced by a text description. On page 12, the entire set of four hexagons containing the core information ('Cost', 'Digital Literacy', 'Relevancy', 'Access') is missing. On page 21, most of the 'Playbook' tiles are missing. These are not decorative images but essential content structures.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 10 shows two blue hexagons with text; Page 12 shows four hexagons with labels ('Cost', 'Digital Literacy', 'Relevancy', 'Access'); Page 21 shows five colorful tiles with labels.
checks.b_evidence_output: On HTML page 10, text description is present but graphics and partial text are missing. On HTML page 12, the section is effectively empty of content besides a logo placeholder. On HTML page 21, several tiles are missing.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'Common Barriers to Broadband Deployment' (page 10), 'Common Barriers to Broadband Adoption' (page 12), and 'The Playbook' (page 21)

checks.a_analysis: No instances of body content duplication were found. Repeated headings (like 'Executive Order 91') correctly correspond to multiple slides in the source material covering the same topic.
